easy.companyDescription: An internet retailer expanding quickly across consumer categories.
easy.macroContext: Internet valuations are peaking as investors start demanding proof of durable profits.
easy.situation: Will scale turn heavy spending into a lasting advantage, or will cash burn collide with tighter capital?
easy.longCase: Revenue is growing rapidly as more shoppers move online, and category expansion could lock in a durable position.
easy.shortCase: Losses are mounting while the stock has already run far ahead of current earnings, making a sentiment reversal costly.
setupHints: Fulfillment, marketing, and expansion spending are rising faster than near-term profit.

medium.companyDescription: A high-growth consumer business reinvesting heavily to expand its reach.
medium.macroContext: After a long stretch of speculative valuations, investors are starting to demand clearer economics.
medium.situation: Will heavy reinvestment create a lasting edge, or will tighter capital expose a fragile model?
medium.longCase: If demand keeps compounding, today's spending could lock in a hard-to-replicate position.
medium.shortCase: If funding becomes scarce before economics improve, an elevated valuation can reverse sharply.

hard.macroContext: After years of risk appetite, capital markets are becoming more selective.
hard.situation: Should investors keep funding multi-year expansion when patience is shorter?
hard.longCase: If demand keeps compounding, early spending can still create a durable competitive position.
hard.shortCase: If funding dries up before economics improve, an elevated valuation can unwind quickly.

reveal.shortText: That was Amazon. The dot-com crash wiped out most of its market value, but it survived to become one of the most valuable companies in the world.
reveal.funFact: Amazon stock fell from about $107 to $7 a share during the bust — a roughly 93% decline.
whyItMoved: Internet valuations collapsed as capital tightened.
Investors demanded a path to profit instead of pure growth.
The stock had already priced aggressive expansion success.

sources.label: Prototype sample seed
sources.notes: Placeholder sample content for Phase 3 pipeline; not production research.

review.reviewNotes: H027 Hard rewrite after H026 Gate 2 identity fail (correct #1 conf 16). Shared-label exception: title The Scale Bet→Capital Weather; decisionDateLabel Dec 1999→1999; era Dot-com bubble and aftermath→Late-1990s equity boom. Hard prose stripped of scale/habit/reinvestment-only silhouette; peer set Amazon/Yahoo/eBay/Cisco/AOL/Priceline. Easy/Medium prose preserved; all Gate 2 entries removed as stale. Follow-up blind judge must rejudge easy+medium+hard.
